title: digitalis purpurea subsp. mariana
intro: Digitalis purpurea subsp. mariana, also known as purple foxglove, is a subspecies of the Digitalis purpurea plant, a biennial herb native to the Pacific coast of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to increase cardiac contractility and slow heart rate, making it useful in treating heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Digitalis purpurea subsp. mariana include improved cardiovascular function, reduced risk of heart failure, and alleviation of symptoms associated with heart conditions. Digitalis purpurea subsp. mariana can be prepared as a herbal tincture, tea, or extract, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
